name: Mahatma Gandhi
facts: Mahatma Gandhi was born in Porbandar, Gujarat, on October 2, 1869.
He pioneered the philosophy of Satyagraha, which emphasizes non-violent resistance to injustice.
He led the famous Dandi March in 1930 to protest against the British salt tax.
His birthday, October 2, is observed as the International Day of Non-Violence by the United Nations.
He was given the title 'Mahatma', meaning 'Great Soul', by Nobel laureate Rabindranath Tagore.
